prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of furan was performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The geometry was the user-supplied furan.xyz file, used without re-optimization. The Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor were computed via finite differences using the neutral molecule and ±1 charged states (both multiplicity 2). All three electronic states converged; charge conservation checks passed. The most electrophilic site is the β-carbon (f⁺ = 1.9243), while the oxygen is strongly nucleophilic (f⁺ = −1.332). The results identify the β-carbons as primary sites for electrophilic aromatic substitution, consistent with experimental furan chemistry. A visualization plot was generated. No convergence issues or warnings reported; integrity status is trustworthy.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">9
